hero.title: About Taytay, Rizal
hero.description: Welcome to the Garment Capital of the Philippines—a vibrant, first-class municipality blending historical roots, booming local industries, and a resilient community.

geography.title: Geography
geography.description: Situated within Rizal province, bordered securely by Pasig City, Cainta, Antipolo City, Angono, and the coast of Laguna de Bay.

population.title: Population
population.description: Home to over 380,000 residents across five highly interconnected, economically vibrant foundational barangays.

industry.title: Garments & Textiles
industry.description: Proudly recognized as the country's textile capital, supplying high-quality, locally tailored garments across the archipelago.

woodworks.title: Woodcraft & Trade
woodworks.description: Renowned for deep heritage in sturdy wood craftsmanship, master carpentry, and custom furniture production lines.

climate.title: Climate
climate.description: Experiences a warm tropical monsoon climate with defined dry intervals from November to April and rainy periods from May to October.

overview.title: Municipal Overview
paragraphs: Taytay is an aggressive first-class municipality positioned on the progressive slopes of Rizal province. Serving as an essential urban gateway linking Eastern Metro Manila to the Calabarzon region, it achieves a sustainable balance between modern commercial industrialization and rich neighborhood identities.

Historically recognized as one of the oldest settlements in the lake-bound area, Taytay successfully made the jump from a peaceful riverside farming community to an urban commercial hub. Today, its dynamic local open markets, or tiangges, drive massive decentralized micro-economies that empower thousands of local entrepreneurs.
